reasoning: The FRED statistical trend analysis gives an estimated probability of 31% for the DEXJPUS exchange rate to increase. Moreover, macroeconomic forecasts broadly expect the US dollar to weaken against the Japanese yen in 2026 due to anticipated Federal Reserve rate cuts (with a notable meeting in mid-March) and potential Bank of Japan rate hikes. Since 2026-03-08 is a short horizon, the probability remains close to the statistical momentum indicator of ~30-35%. For 2026-03-31, despite the fundamental downside pressure on the USD/JPY, we are adhering to the instruction that uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon, bringing the probability closer to 0.5. Therefore, the probabilities are set at 0.34 for Mar 8 and 0.43 for Mar 31.
